name: Herbed Biscuit Egg Bake
recipeInstructions:
1. To make biscuit crust: In a bowl, sift together the flour, baking powder, and salt. Add the garlic powder, lemon pepper, dried thyme, and rosemary. Using a pastry blender or two knives, cut in the shortening using a scissor motion until the mixture is like coarse cornmeal. Make a well in the center; pour in 2/3 cup milk. Mix lightly with a fork, adding enough more milk to form dough that is moist enough to release from the bowl and cling to a fork as a ball. Knead a few times, working gently, until a dough forms.
2. Press the biscuit dough into an 8-inch x 8-inch pan, lining the bottom and sides. If there is a little leftover, it can be used for biscuits baked on the side or as a crust for a smaller individual dish.
3. Lightly cook the chopped onions by steaming, sautéing, or microwaving them for a few minutes. Spread the onion evenly over the dough in the pan.
4. In a bowl, mix together the eggs, dried tarragon, thyme, garlic powder, lemon pepper, and salt (if desired). Pour this mixture into the crust.
5. Add the broccoli florets to the egg mixture in the crust. Sprinkle the grated cheddar cheese over the top. Bake at 400 degrees Fahrenheit for 35-40 minutes.
Note: You can vary the ingredients by using different vegetables, cheeses, or meats in the filling. You can also substitute your favorite herbs in the crust.
